I took my daughter (age 17) to the museum because I thought it might be interesting. Sadly, I was wrong. The majority of displays look like they have not been updated in 50 years. There is a lot of dusty taxidermy. There are a lot of small replica artifacts. The entire museum has a musty smell. My daughter was bored after an hour or so and wanted to leave. Pass on this museum unless you are an archeologist or otherwise have an overwhelming interest in artifacts.

The American Museum of Natural History is one of the best places I visited in New York. I actually went there twice in one week because the museum has such a wide range of exhibits, showcasing different kinds of species and themes at different times. It’s definitely a must-see destination. It’s also incredibly educational and engaging, especially for children, making it a perfect place for families.

We had to catch a flight, so only had about 2-1/2 hours there, not expecting it to be so big. So we only saw the dinosaur age, and gems and mineral exhibits. Both exhibits were excellent. We wish we had longer to see the rest of the museum, so if in New York again, would definitely go back.
The dinosaur and prehistoric fossil section was well laid out and very interesting and informative. The gems and minerals exhibit is the largest and best I’ve ever seen. As I collect gemstones and geodes, it was awesome and we spent a full hour just in that exhibit!
And for those who may need it: they have complimentary wheelchairs to help see everything.
